utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: morning roy , how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , my nurse told you a little bit about dax ? so , if you do n't mind , i'm going to tell dax a little bit about you today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great , great . so , roy is a 50-year-old male patient who comes in with a history of depression , you had some , um , high blood pressure and some hypertension .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , tell me how have you been doing?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure . so it's been a , a pretty stressful winter and spring . and , uh , we've been onboarding a couple new employees and we have been pretty short-staffed in the meantime .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , any changes in , um , weight gain or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably for the last month or so , we've been hosting some friends over vacation , and on ... you know , at the beach in the summer , so the diet is off the charts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . okay . gotcha , gotcha .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: out the window .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: out the window . okay . and , um , now i know you've had a history of depression . you're on zoloft , uh , 50 milligrams a day . have you been doing okay with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , for the most part . again , it's been kind of a challenging year-and-a-half or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i have my bouts . but i- it- it's under control .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , sleeping okay at night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: occasionally ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i , i could use more sleep like everybody .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but, uh, i'll, i'll usually wake up at about 5:00 in the morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: which is a little early .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , no feelings of , uh , you want to hurt yourself or anybody ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good , good . and , um , your high blood pressure , um , that's been under relatively good control recently . have you found that ? your blood pressures have been okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . that's why this is a little concerning to me , because of the , uh , recent , the recent readings .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , gotcha . gotcha . okay . so , um , if you do n't mind , i'm going to do a quick exam . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , um , on my physical exam , your lungs are clear . your heart is , is regular . you do have a grade two systolic ejection murmur . we've had that before , so nothing new-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . there . um , and not clinically relevant at all . um , your extremities are normal , no edema . um , you have put on a c- a , a little bit of weight .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i want you to get back on that diet .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: because that's the key . right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: as far as your high blood pressure , not going to change anything there . okay ? we'll just leave you on your lisinopril 5 milligrams a day , that's good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's also good for your kidneys , so everything , everything good ... is good there . your third problem is your depression . been a rough year . some of that's just normal reactive stuff . so i think we'll just keep you where you are at . not going to change the zoloft . do you need a refill on your zoloft ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , actually i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey dragon , order a zoloft 50 milligrams daily . we'll get that refilled for you . okay ? so , um , i'd like to see you back in about a month , an- and then in about , uh , three months

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a1c , if everything's getting back on track . okay ? anything else i can do for you today ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no . that should do it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . come with me , we'll get you checked out . hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 50-year-old male with a history of depression and hypertension.
Abnormal labs
The patient reports a stressful winter and spring due to being short-staffed at work.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced weight gain.
The patient admits to recent weight gain and poor diet due to hosting friends over the summer.
The patient is taking Zoloft 50 mg daily.
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with his depression medication.
The patient reports the last year and a half has been challenging.
The patient states his depression is under control.
The doctor asks if the patient is sleeping well.
The patient reports waking up at 5:00 a.m.
The doctor asks if the patient has thoughts of self-harm or harming others.
The patient denies thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation.
The doctor asks if the patient's blood pressure has been under control.
The patient expresses concern regarding recent blood pressure readings.
Physical exam findings: lungs clear, heart regular with grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
Physical exam findings: extremities normal with no edema, patient has gained weight.
The doctor advises the patient to return to his diet.
The patient will continue lisinopril 5 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill on Zoloft.
The doctor orders a refill for Zoloft 50 mg daily.
The doctor schedules a follow-up visit in one month and another in three months.
